title: Boone County passes moratorium on data center development
description:
The Boone County Commissioners on Monday approved a one-year moratorium on the development of data centers within unincorporated areas of the county.

title: Mount Baldy reopens after sand management project
description:
The National Park Service said the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers moved 27,000 tons of sand to help address sand accumulation at the site, including portions of the parking area and nearby sanitary infrastructure.

title: Federal customs agents seize 1,578 fake World Cup items in Indianapolis
description:
Indianapolis customs officers seized an estimated $134,000 worth of counterfeit World Cup merchandise earlier this month, officials announced Monday.

title: Dubois County sheriff loses law enforcement certification but keeps elected office
description: The Indiana Law Enforcement Training Board accepted Dubois County Sheriff Thomas Kleinhelter’s voluntary relinquishment of his certification Monday — but the action does not remove the embattled sheriff from his office.

title: NCAA, Big 12 go to court over former IU quarterback with gambling addiction
description: The Indianapolis-based NCAA asked a Texas appeals court Monday to stay a temporary injunction that cleared the way for Texas Tech University quarterback Brendan Sorsby to play this fall despite being declared ineligible

title: Developer plans to remake historic dairy building in Michigan City
description: A Michigan City developer is moving to redevelop a 1930s dairy building in the city’s mid-city corridor into a four-concept commercial space that could include a restaurant, pub, event venue and ice cream parlor. Werner

title: Chore-Time breaks ground on manufacturing expansion in Milford
description:
Milford-based Chore-Time said the expansion to its manufacturing space is expected to be operational by early 2027.

title: Indiana takes on powerful hospitals by capping prices they charge employers
description:
Tired of watching its employers struggle to afford the cost of health care, Republican-controlled Indiana is trying a traditionally liberal tactic to control costs: setting government price controls on hospitals.

title: 12 dead in crash of plane on skydiving outing
description:
A plane carrying a pilot and 11 passengers on a skydiving outing in Missouri crashed in a field and was engulfed in flames Sunday, killing all aboard, authorities said.
